title: Welcome to The Luminous Library
author: Matt
body:
Welcome to The Luminous Library

This is an archive. A living, growing record of thoughts, code, papers, and projects.
A portfolio of a mind that couldn’t stop asking questions, and couldn’t stop building answers.

The Luminous Library exists for one reason, to contribute to humanity’s progress. Not in a grand or self-important way, but in the quiet, persistent way of
someone who believes knowledge should be free, accessible, and useful. For humanity to do better. Have better. Be better.

Think of this as a modern Library of Alexandria, focused on the domains shaping our next century:
technology, psychology, philosophy, physics, AI, cognitive science, systems theory… and whatever else exists between them.

If you’re reading this and something here resonates, if it feels like someone else sees what you see,

That’s why this exists.

Even if it reaches one person.

Even if it shifts one mind by the smallest possible degree.

That’s enough.

That will always be enough.

— Matt D. Myers, Luminal Systems

title: The Myers Fractal & MIP
author: Matt
body: The Myers Fractal is not merely a pattern—it's a manifestation of recursive identity, born from an elegant accident and refined into form. In this post, we explore its shape, mathematical identity, and link to the MIP (Myers Interlock Principle). Includes link to whitepaper and source imagery.

title: The Playground
author: Matt
body: The Playground is where fractals are born and played with in real time. Built entirely in vanilla JS + HTML5 canvas, it lets users toggle 12+ fractals and visualize recursive geometry. This post links to the project and explains its development journey.

title: Less is More — The TLL Blog Project
author: Matt
body: Designed to function without a backend, the TLL Blog is a lightweight CMS inspired by flat file structures. Perfectly suited for GitHub Pages, it supports posts, tags, comments (via JSON), and export/import—all without any server. This post includes the open-source link and implementation details.
